Trump’s strategy has shifted from “Epic Fury” on the military side to “Economic Fury” on the pressure side. That makes a rollback of the Hormuz blockade by May 31 look unlikely, because the blockade is now part of the leverage architecture rather than a temporary wartime move. If the goal is to force Iranian concessions without reopening a major military campaign, keeping maximum economic pressure in place is the rational path.
